chapter: Classification of International Society for the Study of Vascular Anomalies (ISSVA

differentials.name: greater conspicuity with age ← steady growth commensurate with patient growth
differentials.raw: greater conspicuity with age ← steady growth commensurate with patient growth (puberty, pregnancy, trauma, surgery may cause growth spurts)

differentials.name: Kasabach-Merritt syndrome (b)  NO mass = high-flow vascular malformation
differentials.raw: Kasabach-Merritt syndrome (b)  NO mass = high-flow vascular malformation

differentials.name: Vascular metameric syndrome (a)  Wyburn-Mason syndrome (b)  Cobb syndrome
differentials.raw: Vascular metameric syndrome (a)  Wyburn-Mason syndrome (b)  Cobb syndrome

context: =  error of vascular morphogenesis → collection of dysplastic abnormally dilated arteries, veins, capillaries, lymphatics characterized by √  absence of flow voids on SE images DDx:  low-signal-intensity striations, septa, thrombosed vessels, phleboliths (a)  Venous malformation (b)  Lymphatic malformation (c)  Capillary malformation Frequency:   10% of malformations in extremities √  presence of signal voids demonstrating enhancement and high-signal-intensity foci on GRE images (a)  Arteriovenous malformation (b)  Congenital arteriovenous fistula Characterization: √  NO blood flow = lymphatic
